what_it_does: This noticeboard item synthesizes a user's informal graph idea—nodes, observations, and typed edges—and maps it to existing system concepts (addresses, marks, relations), arguing that the dragnet's desired unified relation graph is already latent and waiting to be assembled.
observations: Front matter identifies the item as id item-bd30758f, type block, source claude_code, channel dragnet-development, with a part_of link to board://item-389c8489.
The body claims 19 address kinds resolve through one resolver, equating an address to a graph node.
It says the system already has a concept called 'marks' that are append-only observations stamped on addressed things, with an observation-types registry.
Marks are described as 'mark-is-truth': the underlying thing is not edited; current state is composed by folding observations in order.
Typed edges are equated to a 'relation' registry with direction and inverse; the board already links items across address types.
A traversal function is said to exist in a first form, following typed links outward and resolving targets.
Dragnet index facts are described as three-part subject-relationship-object statements, i.e., graph-shaped raw material.
The system is said to have a tracked, named, not-yet-built design for a unified relation graph with a generalized query language.
The conclusion is that dragnet cards should become observations on file-nodes linked by typed edges, populating the graph the system already intends.
standouts: The user's spontaneous graph vocabulary ('observations', 'typed edges', 'registries-are-also-a-graph') is claimed to converge almost word-for-word with existing system abstractions.
The file states the system has an explicit, tracked, but unbuilt design for exactly this unified relation graph.
It frames marks as the system's literal implementation of 'observations' with a 'mark-is-truth' semantics.
conventions: Uses YAML frontmatter with id, address, type, source, state, title, author_session, channel, links, created/updated timestamps, and history.
Uses board:// URI addressing and a part_of link to a parent item.
Body is Markdown with section headers and bullet lists, using bold for key terms.
Records an interpretive synthesis in the channel-memory noticeboard rather than code or a decision record.
concerns: The text makes strong conclusory claims ('agents found', 'not a coincidence', 'agreed-upon direction') without providing inline evidence or citations in this file.
It blends existing implementation, partial features, and aspirational design, which could be read as overstating how much is already built.
No concrete schema, code identifiers, or document references are included to ground the mapping.
notes: A channel-memory synthesis arguing that a user's graph intuition is already encoded in the system's address/mark/relation machinery and its unbuilt unified-graph roadmap.
questions: What are the 19 address kinds and where is the single resolver defined?
What is the tracked named design document for the unified relation graph?
What is the exact schema of the observation-types and relation-types registries?
How are marks physically stored and folded to compose current state?
What is the traversal function and where does it live?
What is item-389c8489 and how does it relate to this item?
Which agents surfaced these findings and from what sources?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—showing that the graph idea is the system's own unbuilt design—matches what the file does: it maps the user's model to existing concepts and concludes the graph layer is ready to be populated.
apparent_themes: Append-only observations as source of truth
Convergence of human intuition and system design
Building from existing registries rather than inventing new ones
intention_signals: The file appears to be trying to validate a user's mental model by demonstrating it aligns with existing system abstractions, and to make the case that the next graph layer is a natural assembly of parts already present.
novelty: It reframes a spontaneous user idea as a rediscovery of the system's pre-existing, tracked-but-unbuilt design, and explicitly names 'mark-is-truth' and three-part facts as graph raw material.
contribution: It contributes a conceptual bridge between an intuitive graph model and the system's address/mark/relation machinery, plus a recorded statement of intent to build a unified relation graph from existing facts.
summary_for_embedding: A noticeboard item that maps a user's graph-model intuition to the system's existing address, mark, and relation abstractions and frames the dragnet's tracked-but-unbuilt unified relation graph as the natural next layer.
intention_for_embedding: To show that the desired graph representation is not a new architecture but the population of an already-intended graph layer using existing marks and typed relations.
